title: [lichen_l2.c:1565] lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort precondition check uses wrong API
description:
Contract: lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort should only be called from deinit() when module is in DEINITING state.
Violation: The precondition check (line 1612) uses is_running() which returns false for STOPPED, DEINITING, ABORTED, and UNINIT states. It cannot distinguish between valid calls (DEINITING) and invalid calls (STOPPED, UNINIT). The comment (lines 1597-1610) acknowledges this as an 'architectural limitation'.
Impact: Medium - Calling from STOPPED state would reinitialize a valid mutex (harmless but wasteful). Calling from UNINIT would reinitialize an uninitialized mutex (undefined, but k_mutex_init on static mutex is likely safe). The only dangerous case (RUNNING) is correctly rejected.
Mitigation: The comment documents the limitation adequately. Could add lora_l2 API to query exact state if stricter checking needed.
close_reason: The is_running() check is correct. The comment (lines 1643-1661) thoroughly documents this as an architectural limitation and explains why it is acceptable: (1) The dangerous case (RUNNING) is correctly rejected; (2) Other states (STOPPED/UNINIT) result in harmless mutex reinit; (3) The function is internal-only with a single caller (deinit); (4) Adding is_deiniting() API would expand API surface for a single caller. No code change needed - the limitation is properly documented and the check is sufficient.
